target.name: Q-Factor
target.founder: Nir Davidson, Ofer Firstenberg, Yoav Sagi, Guy Raz

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $24M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Quantum Computing

data.narrative:
Q-Factor is a seed-stage quantum computing company that has raised $24M to date. The quantum computing sector includes 3 public comparables (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers, among them IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum, and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis is applicable and serves as the primary valuation method. Using total capital raised of $24M as a proxy for current valuation and applying a typical 2.5x multiplier for progression from seed to the subsequent stage yields an implied valuation of $60M. Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables within quantum computing at the same or adjacent stage with similar valuation tiers; while 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints when filtered by stage and tier proximity.

The triangulated valuation range is $51M (low) to $69M (high), with a median of $60M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty around stage-transition multiples, which can vary materially by investor cohort and market conditions. The absence of strong peer-level data in quantum computing at this stage limits precision. Key sensitivities include the company's technical progress toward prototype demonstration and capital efficiency relative to sector norms.
